raw_output_payload.reasoning: Need to echo date and time then " 1" and append to file. Use date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S' maybe. Command: echo "$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') 1" >> /system/folder3/temp/empty.txt. Also print to console: echo ... ; echo ... >> file. But requirement: write current date/time followed by " 1" to console and append to file. So two commands: echo ... ; echo ... >> file. But they want one bash command. Could use tee -a. echo "$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') 1" | tee -a /system/folder3/temp/empty.txt. That prints to console and appends. So answer.
